description:
## What

`build_fn_signature` (src/ir_codegen.rs:708) appends `host_ctx: i64` to every native fn's signature unconditionally. For `add1_s2` and `k_2_s3` in fixtures/add1.fz, the body never reads it ā€” `v1: i64` shows up in `block0(v0, v1)` but no instruction references it.

Compute a per-FnId set `fns_needing_host_ctx` via module-wide fixed point and trim the sig (and every caller's arg list) for native fns that don't transitively need it.

## When a native fn needs host_ctx

- Body has `Term::Halt(_)` ā€” `fz_halt` takes host_ctx as its first arg.
- Body has `Term::Call(callee)` or `Term::TailCall(callee)` where `needs_host_ctx(callee)` ā€” the call forwards host_ctx.
- Body has the `emit_halt_and_return_null` path (.27.18) ā€” calls `fz_halt`. (Currently this fires only for uniform fns; left in the analysis for completeness so a future native-entry change doesn't silently regress.)

Fixed point iteration: seed with Halt-having fns, expand via callee transitivity.

## Codegen changes

1. Compute `fns_needing_host_ctx: HashSet<FnId>` in `compile_with_backend` after `natively_callable` is finalized.
2. `build_fn_signature` takes an extra `needs_host_ctx: bool` and only appends the host_ctx AbiParam when true (for native fns; uniform fns always include it because the trampoline ABI is fixed).
3. In `compile_fn`'s native branch, read `host_ctx` from `params` only when this fn needs it. Make the internal `host_ctx` value `Option<ir::Value>`; downstream uses (`fz_halt`, forwarded-to-callee) `.expect()` with site-specific messages ā€” invariant-enforcing same as .27.16.
4. Update every native-call emission site:
   - `Term::Call(native_callee)` native-chain branch (line ~2733): push host_ctx onto `native_args` only if `fns_needing_host_ctx.contains(callee)`.
   - `Term::TailCall(native_callee)` (line ~2929): same.
   - Closure stub's native-callee path (line ~3340): same.
5. Adjust `Signature` for native-fn FuncRefs at call sites accordingly (declare_func_in_func sees the trimmed sig).

## Uniform fns

Stay unchanged. `build_fn_signature(is_native=false, ...)` keeps `(frame_ptr, host_ctx) -> i64` because the trampoline transmutes every dispatch through that sig (src/ir_codegen.rs:216). Trimming uniform sigs requires the entry-harness refactor in .27.20.

## Tests (TDD)

1. CLIF assertion on fixtures/add1.fz: `add1_s2` and `k_2_s3` have signatures `(i64) -> i64 tail` (one param + return), not `(i64, i64) -> i64 tail`. Their `block0` is `block0(v0: i64):` ā€” single param.
2. Behavioural: add1.fz prints 42.
3. Regression net: existing fact / map / sum tests (all of which use Term::Call between native fns) still pass ā€” host_ctx forwarding via the fixed point should keep it in chains that reach `fz_halt`.

## Acceptance

- `cargo test` green, no warnings.
- add1.fz CLIF: `add1_s2` and `k_2_s3` sigs drop the host_ctx i64.
- main still calls them correctly (one arg + the int payload, not two).

## Risks / verification done

- The fixed point is small and finite (call graph nodes).
- Native fns only call native fns (per natively_callable invariant), so the callee transitivity is well-defined.
- The closure stub is a separate Cranelift fn; its sig is set in stub_sigs (line ~1768 area) ā€” needs updating in lockstep.
- `fz_print_i64`, `fz_print_f64`, `fz_print_bool`, `fz_print_atom`, `fz_print_nil` are runtime fns with fixed sigs that don't take host_ctx; they're not affected.
- `fz_halt` and `fz_print_value` (polymorphic) and `fz_alloc_*` etc. do take host_ctx; not affected (they're called from compile_fn with the right arg lists).
title: Trim unused host_ctx from native fn signatures
